steps.title: Join the lifespan contrast
steps.content: The sentence contrasts two complete ideas: calico rockfish live a little over a decade, and rougheye rockfish can live about two centuries. The word while means whereas, so it should join the contrast with a comma.

steps.title: Reject punctuation that fragments
steps.content: Choice A uses a colon incorrectly before while. Choice B starts a new sentence with while but leaves only a dependent clause. Choice C uses a semicolon before the dependent while-clause. The remaining choice uses comma plus while to connect the contrast. The answer is D.
